title: Basics of English — Complete Grammar Reference
topic: Grammar Foundations: Everything You Need to Know
description: यह Day 1 की complete grammar reference है। इसमें 20+ sections हैं जो English grammar की foundation को cover करते हैं। हर section में detailed explanation, formula, examples, और common errors हैं।

sections.title: Parts of Speech — English के 8 Building Blocks
sections.theory:
English के हर word को 8 categories में रखा जा सकता है। इन्हें Parts of Speech कहते हैं।

1. NOUN (संज्ञा): Name of person, place, thing, idea
   Examples: Ram, Delhi, book, love, freedom
   Types: Common, Proper, Abstract, Collective, Material

2. PRONOUN (सर्वनाम): Words replacing nouns
   Examples: I, you, he, she, it, we, they, me, him, her
   Types: Personal, Possessive, Reflexive, Demonstrative

3. VERB (क्रिया): Action or state word
   Examples: eat, sleep, run, is, have, think
   Types: Action verbs, State verbs, Auxiliary verbs, Modal verbs

4. ADJECTIVE (विशेषण): Describes a noun
   Examples: beautiful, tall, red, intelligent, three
   Types: Descriptive, Quantitative, Demonstrative, Possessive

5. ADVERB (क्रिया विशेषण): Describes a verb, adjective, or another adverb
   Examples: quickly, very, here, always, quite
   Types: Manner, Time, Place, Frequency, Degree

6. PREPOSITION (सम्बन्ध बोधक अव्यय): Shows relationship between words
   Examples: in, on, at, to, from, with, for, by, under, above

7. CONJUNCTION (समुच्चयबोधक): Joins words, phrases, clauses
   Examples: and, but, or, because, although, if, when
   Types: Coordinating (FANBOYS), Subordinating, Correlative

8. INTERJECTION (विस्मयादिबोधक): Expresses emotion
   Examples: Wow!, Oh!, Alas!, Hurray!, Oops!

examples.sentence: The beautiful girl reads a book quickly.
examples.breakdown: The(article) beautiful(adj) girl(noun) reads(verb) a(article) book(noun) quickly(adverb)

examples.sentence: The cat sat on the mat.
examples.breakdown: The(article) cat(noun) sat(verb) on(preposition) the(article) mat(noun)

keyRules: एक word different contexts में different parts of speech हो सकता है: 'run' (verb: I run), 'run' (noun: a run)
Adjective noun को modify करता है, adverb verb/adjective/adverb को
Prepositions हमेशा object के साथ आती हैं: 'in the room', 'at home'
Articles (a, an, the) technically determiners/adjectives हैं
हर sentence में कम से कम एक noun/pronoun और एक verb होती है

sections.title: Types of Sentences — 4 Main Types
sections.theory:
English में 4 main types of sentences होते हैं:

1. DECLARATIVE (Statement) — Something बताने के लिए
   Full stop (.) से end होता है
   Example: I study English every day.
   Hindi: मैं रोज़ English पढ़ता हूँ।

2. INTERROGATIVE (Question) — कुछ पूछने के लिए
   Question mark (?) से end होता है
   Yes/No: Do you study English?
   WH: What do you study?

3. IMPERATIVE (Command/Request) — कुछ करने को कहने के लिए
   Verb से शुरू होता है, subject 'you' implied
   Command: Study English now!
   Request: Please help me.
   Negative: Don't be late.

4. EXCLAMATORY — Strong emotion express करने के लिए
   Exclamation mark (!) से end होता है
   'What' या 'How' से शुरू होता है
   Example: What a beautiful day!
   Example: How wonderful this is!
keyRules: Declarative: Subject + Verb + Object.
Interrogative: Auxiliary + Subject + Main Verb?
Imperative: Verb (base form)! [subject 'you' hidden]
Exclamatory: What + a/an + adj + noun! OR How + adj/adv + subject + verb!

sections.title: Tense Overview — Simple, Continuous, Perfect
sections.theory:
English में 3 main times हैं:
1. PAST — Jo ho gaya (हो चुका)
2. PRESENT — Jo ho raha hai (हो रहा है/होता है)
3. FUTURE — Jo hoga (होगा)

Har time के 4 aspects hote hain:
- Simple (सामान्य)
- Continuous / Progressive (जारी)
- Perfect (पूर्ण)
- Perfect Continuous (पूर्ण+जारी)

Total = 3 × 4 = 12 Tenses

DAY 1 FOCUS: Simple Present Tense
Simple Present ka use:
• Regular/habitual actions: I eat rice every day.
• Facts/general truths: The sun rises in the east.
• Permanent situations: She lives in Mumbai.
• Schedules/timetables: The train leaves at 9 AM.
• Stative verbs (know, love, have, understand, believe, want)
keyRules: Simple Present: I/We/You/They + V1; He/She/It + V1+s/es
Time markers help identify tense — always look for them
Simple Present ≠ Present Continuous — Simple = routine, Continuous = ongoing NOW
Stative verbs (know, love, want, have, understand) don't use Continuous
Day 1 focus: Master Simple Present first — it's used in 60% of English sentences

sections.title: Nouns — Count vs Uncountable, Plural Forms
sections.theory:
Nouns दो main types की होती हैं:

1. COUNTABLE NOUNS — जिन्हें गिना जा सके
   Singular: a book, a chair, one apple
   Plural: books, chairs, apples
   Plural Rules:
   • Regular: + s → book → books
   • -sh/-ch/-x/-z/-s: + es → box → boxes, watch → watches
   • Consonant + y → ies: city → cities, baby → babies
   • -f/-fe → ves: leaf → leaves, wife → wives
   • Irregular: man → men, woman → women, child → children, tooth → teeth, foot → feet, mouse → mice

2. UNCOUNTABLE NOUNS — जिन्हें गिना न जा सके
   No plural form!
   Examples: water, milk, sugar, rice, information, advice, news, furniture, luggage, weather
   WRONG: informations, advices, furnitures
   RIGHT: some information, a piece of advice

IMPORTANT: Quantity expressions with uncountable nouns:
   • a piece of advice/furniture/information
   • a bottle of water/milk
   • a cup of tea/coffee
   • a bag of rice/sugar
   • a bit of news

examples.correct: He has a lot of luggage.

examples.note: Water = uncountable → no article needed for general statement

keyRules: News, information, furniture, luggage, advice — ALWAYS uncountable
Use 'much/less/a little' with uncountable; 'many/fewer/a few' with countable
Irregular plural: man/men, child/children, tooth/teeth, foot/feet
-y after consonant → -ies: city/cities, story/stories
-f/-fe → -ves: leaf/leaves, knife/knives, life/lives

sections.title: Adjectives — Types and Order in English
sections.theory:
Adjectives noun को describe करते हैं। English में adjective ALWAYS noun से BEFORE आता है (Hindi के उलट):

Hindi: एक लंबा आदमी (tall man)
English: A tall man (NOT: A man tall)

ADJECTIVE TYPES:
1. Descriptive: beautiful, tall, red, intelligent, heavy
2. Quantitative: some, many, few, much, several, all
3. Demonstrative: this, that, these, those
4. Possessive: my, your, his, her, its, our, their
5. Interrogative: which, what, whose
6. Numeral: one, first, second, double, triple
7. Comparative: taller, more beautiful, better
8. Superlative: tallest, most beautiful, best

ADJECTIVE ORDER (when multiple adjectives are used):
Opinion → Size → Age → Shape → Color → Origin → Material → Purpose + NOUN
Example: 'A beautiful (opinion) little (size) old (age) round (shape) green (color) French (origin) silver (material) decorating (purpose) box'

Most common combinations:
• size + color: a big red car
• opinion + size: a wonderful small cottage
• age + material: an old wooden table

examples.hindi: यह एक छोटी पुरानी किताब है।
examples.english: This is a small old book.

keyRules: Adjective हमेशा noun से BEFORE आता है (not after like Hindi)
Multiple adjectives: opinion-size-age-shape-color-origin-material
After be-verb (is/am/are): subject + is/am/are + adjective: She is beautiful
Comparative: adjective + er / more + adjective → taller, more intelligent
Superlative: the + adjective + est / the most + adjective → the tallest, the most intelligent

sections.title: Basic Prepositions — in, on, at, to, from, with, by, for
sections.theory:
Prepositions सबसे confusing part of English हैं क्योंकि Hindi में इनके exact equivalents नहीं होते।

TIME Prepositions:
• AT — specific time: at 5 PM, at midnight, at noon
• ON — days/dates: on Monday, on 15 July, on my birthday
• IN — months/years/seasons/longer periods: in July, in 2024, in summer, in the morning

PLACE Prepositions:
• AT — specific point: at home, at the station, at the corner
• ON — surface: on the table, on the wall, on the floor
• IN — enclosed space: in the room, in the bag, in India

MOVEMENT Prepositions:
• TO — direction: I go to school
• FROM — origin: I come from Delhi
• INTO — movement inside: She walked into the room
• ONTO — movement on surface: He climbed onto the roof

OTHER COMMON Prepositions:
• WITH — company/tool: She came with me; Cut with a knife
• FOR — purpose/duration: This is for you; I waited for 2 hours
• BY — agent/means/deadline: Written by Shakespeare; Travel by train; Submit by Friday
• ABOUT — topic: We talked about cricket
• BETWEEN — two things: Between you and me
• AMONG — more than two: Among the students

examples.english: I go to office at 9 AM.
The book is on the table.
The key is in the bag.

keyRules: AT = specific point (time/place); ON = day/date/surface; IN = period/enclosed
Never say 'in 5 PM' or 'on the morning' — AT 5 PM, IN the morning
Time: at noon/midnight, on Sunday/Christmas Day, in the morning/2024/winter
Transport: by bus/train/car/plane (NOT: with bus, in train)
Arrive AT small places, arrive IN large places: arrive at school, arrive in Delhi

sections.title: Conjunctions — FANBOYS and Subordinating
sections.theory:
Conjunctions sentences को join करते हैं।

1. COORDINATING CONJUNCTIONS — FANBOYS
   F - For (because): I left early, for I was tired.
   A - And (addition): I eat rice and dal.
   N - Nor (neither/nor): I don't drink, nor do I smoke.
   B - But (contrast): He is poor but happy.
   O - Or (choice): Tea or coffee?
   Y - Yet (however): She is tired, yet she keeps working.
   S - So (result): It was raining, so I took an umbrella.

2. SUBORDINATING CONJUNCTIONS — Create dependent clauses
   Time: when, while, before, after, since, until, as soon as
   Reason: because, since, as
   Condition: if, unless, provided that
   Contrast: although, though, even though, while
   Purpose: so that, in order that

3. CORRELATIVE CONJUNCTIONS — Pairs
   Both...and: Both Ram and Shyam came.
   Either...or: Either you or I am wrong.
   Neither...nor: Neither he nor she knows.
   Not only...but also: He is not only intelligent but also hardworking.

examples.hindi: मैं tired था, इसलिए घर चला गया।
examples.english: I was tired, so I went home.

examples.hindi: वो talented है, लेकिन lazy भी।
अगर तुम practice करोगे, तो improve करोगे।
examples.english: If you practice, you will improve.

examples.hindi: जब तक वो नहीं आता, हम wait करेंगे।
examples.english: We will wait until he comes.

examples.hindi: वो intelligent और hardworking दोनों है।
examples.english: He is both intelligent and hardworking.

keyRules: Coordinating conjunctions join equal elements (words, phrases, clauses)
Subordinating conjunctions create a clause that depends on main clause
Comma before coordinating conjunction when joining two complete sentences
FANBOYS: For, And, Nor, But, Or, Yet, So — memorize these!
'Because' के बाद reason आता है; 'But' opposite idea आती है

sections.title: Be Verb — am, is, are (Present)
sections.theory:
Be verb English की सबसे fundamental verb है। यह subject के अनुसार change होती है:

Subject → Be Verb:
I → am
He/She/It/Singular Noun → is
You/We/They/Plural Noun → are

USES:
1. Identity: I am a student. She is a doctor.
2. Profession: He is an engineer. They are teachers.
3. Age: I am 25 years old.
4. Appearance: She is beautiful. He is tall.
5. State/Feeling: I am happy. She is tired.
6. Location: He is at home. They are in Mumbai.
7. Nationality: I am Indian. She is American.

NEGATIVE:
I am not (I'm not) = मैं नहीं हूँ
He/She/It is not (He's not / He isn't) = वो नहीं है
You/We/They are not (aren't) = तुम/हम/वो नहीं हैं

QUESTION:
Am I...? Are you...? Is he/she/it...? Are we/they...?
(Just put Am/Is/Are at the start)

SHORT ANSWERS:
Are you a teacher? Yes, I am. / No, I'm not.
Is she happy? Yes, she is. / No, she isn't.
keyRules: I → am (always — no exception)
He/She/It/Singular → is
You/We/They/Plural → are
Short forms: I'm, He's, She's, It's, We're, You're, They're
Questions: put Am/Is/Are at the very beginning
Negative: add 'not' after Am/Is/Are

sections.title: There is / There are — Existence Sentences
sections.hindiTitle: There is / There are — अस्तित्व दर्शाना
sections.theory:
When we want to say something EXISTS somewhere, we use 'There is' or 'There are'.

There is + singular/uncountable noun
There are + plural noun

Examples:
There is a book on the table. (मेज़ पर एक किताब है।)
There are many students in the class. (क्लास में बहुत छात्र हैं।)
There is some milk in the glass. (गिलास में कुछ दूध है।)
There is no problem. (कोई problem नहीं है।)
There are no apples left. (कोई apple नहीं बचा।)

QUESTION FORM:
Is there a problem? (कोई problem है क्या?)
Are there any buses? (कोई bus है क्या?)

NEGATIVE:
There is no... / There isn't any...
There are no... / There aren't any...

PAST:
There was / There were (same rules, past tense)

examples.english: There is a chair in the room.
There was water in the glass.

keyRules: There is = singular/uncountable; There are = plural
Question: Is there...? / Are there...?
Negative: There is no + noun / There isn't a + noun / There aren't any + noun
Past: There was / There were
There is और It is में difference: There is = existence; It is = specific thing

sections.title: Modal Verbs Introduction — Can, Could, Will, Would, Should, Must, May
sections.theory:
Modal verbs special helping verbs हैं। ये possibility, ability, permission, obligation express करते हैं।

Modal Verbs की विशेषताएँ:
1. Modal के बाद ALWAYS base verb (no s/es, no -ing)
2. Modal का question बनाने के लिए Do/Does नहीं लगाते
3. Modal का negative: modal + not

CAN — ability/possibility
• Ability: I can speak English.
• Permission: Can I come in?
• Request: Can you help me?

COULD — past ability/polite request
• Past ability: He could run fast when young.
• Polite request: Could you pass the salt?

WILL — future/willingness
• Future: I will call you tomorrow.
• Willingness: I will help you.

WOULD — polite/conditional
• Polite: Would you like tea?
• Conditional: If I had money, I would buy a car.

SHOULD — advice/obligation
• Advice: You should study daily.
• Obligation: We should respect elders.

MUST — strong obligation/necessity
• Obligation: You must wear a seatbelt.
• Necessity: I must finish this today.

MAY — formal permission/possibility
• Permission: May I come in?
• Possibility: It may rain today.

examples.hindi: क्या मैं अंदर आ सकता हूँ?
examples.english: May I come in? / Can I come in?

examples.hindi: तुम्हें time पर आना ज़रूरी है।

keyRules: Modal + base verb: can go, will come, should study (NEVER can goes, will came)
Modal questions: Can you? Will you? (NOT: Do you can? Does she will?)
Modal negatives: cannot/can't, will not/won't, should not/shouldn't, must not/mustn't
'Can' informal permission; 'May' formal permission
'Must not' = forbidden; 'don't have to' = not necessary (big difference!)

sections.title: Question Tags — Short Confirmation Questions
sections.theory:
Question tags वो short questions हैं जो sentence के end में add करते हैं confirmation या agreement के लिए।

RULE: Positive statement → Negative tag
Negative statement → Positive tag

Examples:
+ She is beautiful, isn't she?
- He doesn't study, does he?
+ They can swim, can't they?
- You haven't eaten, have you?

HOW TO FORM:
1. Positive sentence → negative tag: She works, doesn't she? / They are here, aren't they?
2. Negative sentence → positive tag: He isn't coming, is he? / You don't like it, do you?
3. Use same auxiliary as main sentence
4. Tag pronoun matches subject

SPECIAL CASES:
I am late, aren't I? (not amn't I)
Let's go, shall we?
Do it, will you? (imperative)
Everyone came, didn't they? (Everyone → they in tag)
Nothing happened, did it? (Nothing → it in tag)

examples.tag: They are not here, are they?

keyRules: Positive statement → negative tag (adds n't/not)
Negative statement → positive tag (no n't)
Tag pronoun = subject pronoun (she/he/it/they/you/we)
I am → aren't I? (special case)
Rising intonation = genuine question; Falling intonation = expecting agreement

sections.title: Subject-Verb Agreement — Number and Person Agreement
sections.theory:
Subject और Verb का number (singular/plural) और person match करना ज़रूरी है।

BASIC RULES:
1. Singular subject → singular verb (+ s/es for 3rd person)
   She works. He lives. The team is playing.

2. Plural subject → plural verb
   They work. We live. The teams are playing.

3. Compound subjects with 'and' → plural verb
   Ram and Shyam are friends.

4. Either...or / Neither...nor → verb agrees with closer subject
   Either he or I am wrong.
   Neither the students nor the teacher is here.

5. Collective nouns → usually singular
   The team is ready. (team acts as one)
   The team are arguing. (members acting individually)

6. Indefinite pronouns → mostly singular
   Everyone is here. Nobody knows. Something is wrong.
   Some, Any, All, None → can be singular or plural based on noun

7. Distances/Amounts/Periods as subjects → singular
   Ten kilometers is a long distance.
   Two hours is not enough.
   Five hundred rupees is a lot.

8. 'News' → always singular
   The news is bad. (NOT: The news are bad.)

examples.wrong: The team are going to win.
examples.correct: The team is going to win.

examples.wrong: Ram and Shyam is my friend.
examples.correct: Ram and Shyam are my friends.

keyRules: Singular subject → singular verb; Plural subject → plural verb
'And' usually creates plural subject
Either/or, Neither/nor → verb agrees with CLOSER subject
Everyone, nobody, somebody, everything → singular
Collective nouns (team, family, group) → singular when acting as one

sections.title: Direct vs Indirect Speech Introduction
sections.theory:
Direct Speech: किसी की exact words quotation marks में
Indirect Speech: किसी की words अपने शब्दों में (no quotation marks)

DIRECT: She said, 'I am happy.'
INDIRECT: She said that she was happy.

CHANGES IN INDIRECT SPEECH:
1. Quotation marks remove
2. 'that' add करो (optional for statements)
3. Pronoun changes:
   I → he/she; we → they; you → he/she/they; my → his/her
4. Tense changes (backshift):
   is → was
   am → was
   are → were
   have/has → had
   will → would
   can → could
   may → might
5. Time expression changes:
   today → that day
   yesterday → the previous day / the day before
   tomorrow → the next day / the following day
   here → there
   this → that
   these → those
   now → then

QUESTION in Indirect:
Direct: He asked, 'Do you speak English?'
Indirect: He asked whether/if I spoke English.
(No inversion, whether/if added, question mark removed)

examples.indirect: She said that she loved English.
He said that he was tired.
She asked if I could help her.
He said that he would come the next day.

keyRules: Reporting verb past tense → main verb shifts back one tense
Present → Past; Past → Past Perfect; Will → Would; Can → Could
Pronouns और time expressions change according to context
Questions: whether/if use करो, question mark remove, normal word order
Commands: told/asked + object + to + base verb

goldenRules: SVO order: Subject → Verb → Object — यह English का सबसे basic rule है
He/She/It के साथ Simple Present में verb + s/es
Questions में Do/Does subject से पहले आता है
Articles: a (consonant sound), an (vowel sound), the (specific)
Modal verbs के बाद ALWAYS base verb
Negative: I don't / He doesn't + base verb
Frequency adverbs: before main verb, after be-verb
English में translation से बचो — directly English में सोचो
